pattern: One replacement can stitch together a non-decreasing prefix and suffix only when the gap it bridges is itself non-decreasing, which a linear scan of run lengths can decide.
hints: Without any replacement the answer is just the longest already non-decreasing run; the replacement buys you one extra repair.
Replacing a single element lets you merge the non-decreasing run ending just before it with the run starting just after it, but only if the two surrounding values are compatible.

Track for every index the non-decreasing run ending there and the run starting there; at each i consider sacrificing nums[i] to join left run with the right run starting at i+1 when nums[i-1] <= nums[i+1].

edge_inputs.description: strictly decreasing, only neighbors can merge
one dip repaired by raising it
the replaced element bridges two runs
two separate dips, only one fixable
dip where neighbors are incompatible so it cannot bridge

stress.description: small-range integers so non-decreasing runs and dips are frequent, short enough for the quadratic oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 800))
    nums = [rng.randint(-5, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
